presentation.chiefComplaint: Just feeling weak and off for a day

history.hpi: An elderly woman brought by family for feeling 'washed out' and not herself since yesterday. No localizing complaint at first; on questioning she has had mild burning with urination and one episode of feeling cold and shivery. Family notes she is a little more muddled than usual and has eaten little. She looks deceptively well and is conversational.
history.socialHistory: Lives with her daughter; normally fully independent.
redFlags: Subtle altered mental status from baseline (more muddled than usual)
Occult infection source: dysuria with rigors in a diabetic
Early sepsis despite near-normal-looking vitals (immunosenescence blunts the response)
Borderline tachypnea is the earliest objective sepsis sign

redFlagConcepts.flag: Subtle altered mental status from baseline (more muddled than usual)
Occult infection source: dysuria with rigors in a diabetic
Early sepsis despite near-normal-looking vitals (immunosenescence blunts the response)
Borderline tachypnea is the earliest objective sepsis sign

expert.esiRationale: The trap: vitals look almost reassuring (HR 96, SBP 108, temp 37.6) and she is conversational, so she is easily under-triaged to ESI 3. But she is an elderly diabetic with a new altered mental status, an occult urinary source with rigors, and a danger-zone respiratory rate (22) — the earliest objective sign of evolving sepsis. This is a high-risk, time-critical presentation (sepsis bundle) that should not wait: ESI 2. Under-triage delays antibiotics and fluids while she decompensates.

provenance.sourceRef: ED Triage Trainer hand-authored calibration-trap case
